arts.ttl: Fundraiser set up to help record-breaking former Aston Villa captain
arts.sum: A fundraiser has been set up to raise money to keep a former Aston Villa captain from spending the rest of his life confined to a wheelchair. "Jimmy lived in Birmingham for a big chunk of his post-playing career before moving out to Spain seven years ago," A JustGiving page set up to help the former Villa captain explained.

arts.ttl: Everton and Brighton could be priced out of move for 23-year-old – Price tag just keeps rising
arts.sum: Everton and Brighton and Hove Albion may find themselves priced out of a move for VfB Stuttgart striker Nick Woltemade, as they now value him at €40-50m. That’s at least if the latest from SportBILD is to believed, with them covering the striker today and again insisting that Everton and Brighton are eyeing a move The post Everton and Brighton could be priced out of move for 23-year-old – Price tag just keeps rising appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa XI vs Brighton – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Aston Villa head to Brighton in the Premier League this evening with Champions League qualification in their sights. Just five points separate the teams from fourth to 10th in the Premier League, with Brighton and Villa sandwiched in the chasing pack. A win for Unai Emery’s side tonight will take them above the Seagulls in […] The post Aston Villa XI vs Brighton – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Analysis: Why Joao Pedro is a perfect fit for Brighton's attack
arts.sum: Since arriving in English football, Joao Pedro has scored 16 of the 17 penalties he has taken in all competitions for Watford and Brighton, sending most opposing goalkeepers the wrong way with his cool, side-footed strikes. Joao Pedro has started 20 Premier League matches this season and he made two of his three substitute outings while returning from injury.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa transfer key as club secure healthy profit – And they’re due for more in future
arts.sum: Andrés García’s move to Aston Villa in the January transfer window has helped former club Levante post profits in it’s accounts for the year. That’s according to Marca, who report that García’s move, and a couple of others means the Spanish side ‘could reach a surplus’ in their accounts.
